By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Mami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |所在地=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Mami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |所在地=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Mami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |所在地=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Mami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |所在地=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Mami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |出土品=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji ware]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map2 = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue ware]]-style Haji ware.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Cultural Heritage of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue ware indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links ==
