But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun |Group={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite revision.comment: Text replacement - "|Group" to "| part_of"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield shape. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-typed mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-typed kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-typed mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-typed, square-typed, and round-typed mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-typed haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-typed haniwa with small shields, shield-typed haniwa, and lid-typed haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-typed haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun}}{{Small|{{National Land image Information (Color Aerial Photographs)}}.}]]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |address=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.}]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Cultural Properties of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Cultural Properties Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Cultural Properties Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|j ... 9 KB (1,000 words) - Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|j ... 9 KB (1,000 words) - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|j ... 9 KB (1,000 words) - 07:55, [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]]

The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |別名=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |規模=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |築造時期=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |特記事項=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is designated as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following dimensions: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. Artifacts recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf other_designation of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively designated as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[other_designation of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[other_designation Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=other_designation Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == Notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for other_designation "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]][[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words) -
