{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}

revision.comment: 47 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町) up to rev 104091592
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |画像=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |名称=大塚山古墳 |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（北群うち大塚山古墳群） |別名=川合大塚山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]川合 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.4 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 44|経度秒 = 34.1 |ISO = JP-29 |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長215m（197m）
高さ16.9m（前方部） |築造時期=[[5世紀]]後半 |出土品=[[埴輪]]・[[土師器]] |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「大塚山古墳」
（「大塚山古墳群」に包含） |特記事項=全国第27位の規模[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=大塚山古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=奈良県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|{{center|大塚山古墳の空中写真}}{{Small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] '''大塚山古墳''' （おおつかやまこふん、'''川合大塚山古墳'''）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[河合町]]川合にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（北群のうち大塚山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大塚山古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == *全長215メートル{{Sfn|大塚|小林|熊野|1989|p=117}}または197メートル[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html 古墳] - 河合町 *後円部径108メートル、高さ15.8メートル *前方部幅123メートル、高さ16.9メートル 河合町川合に所在する、前方部を南に向けた三段築成の前方後円墳である。墳丘の保存状態は良好で、周濠跡は水田になっているが、盾形の周濠の痕跡を明瞭に残している。 馬見古墳群の中ではその北部に立地する大塚山古墳群に含まれる。大塚山古墳群は前方後円墳、[[方墳]]、[[円墳]]など8基の古墳から構成されるが、本古墳はその規模の上からも中心的な位置を占めている。 調査が行われていないため埋葬施設は不明である。墳丘には[[葺石]]および[[円筒埴輪]]の囲繞が確認されている。[[遺物]]は、円筒埴輪・[[朝顔形埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（家形埴輪の円柱部分に小型の盾を付けたもの・盾形埴輪・蓋形埴輪）・須恵器模倣[[土師器]]などが確認されている[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf 河合町北東部の文化財案内]（河合町サイト）。須恵質土器が含まれていることから、5世紀中頃～後半の築造と推定され、同時期では[[奈良盆地]]内で最大級の古墳である。 1956年（昭和31年）12月28日、周辺の古墳7基を含め「大塚山古墳群」として国の史跡に指定された。 Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|後円部墳頂 Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|前方部墳頂の大元帥陛下駐蹕之處碑 Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|周濠 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|家形埴輪片（盾付き円柱）
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 出来事 == 2020年（令和2年）4月27日、墳丘上の一部が何者かによって掘り返され、円筒埴輪2本分が持ち去られているのが発見された。内1本は5月1日までの間に墳丘上に破片の状態で戻された。また5月28日までの間に、もう1本が接着剤で接合して組み立てられた状態で墳丘上に戻されていた。国や自治体の指定を受けた[[史跡]]に対するこのような行為（盗掘や採集、原位置からの移動）は、保護のためにその姿や形・状態を変えてはいけないと定められている[[文化財]]を毀損するもの（許可を得ない現状の変更=破壊）であり、[[文化財保護法]]（第125条・196条・197条の1）違反事件として捜査が行われていた{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[日本経済新聞]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=文化財保護法|accessdate=2020-06-19}}。 同年6月12日、町教育委員会はタケノコ掘りをしていた地元の子供とその保護者たちによる仕業だったと発表した{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[朝日新聞]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}}。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=大塚|first=初重|authorlink=大塚初重|last2=小林|first2=三郎|authorlink2=小林三郎 (考古学者)|last3=熊野|first3=正也|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 史跡大塚山古墳群整備基本計画書]|publisher=河合町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[島の山古墳]] * [[小東荘]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1995|大塚山古墳群}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつかやまこふん かわい}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:河合町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

高さ16.9m（前方部） |築造時期=[[5世紀]]後半 |出土品=[[埴輪]]・[[土師器]] |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「大塚山古墳」

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{citation needed|Otsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]]

Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]]

By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]]

By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * {{ill|Shimanoyama Kofun|ja|島の山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, spanning {{ill|Kawai, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kashiba|ja|香芝市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|{{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] ...is exemplified by {{ill|Otomeyama Kofun|ja|乙女山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|河合町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja| ... 9 KB (1,000 words)}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]]
