So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:河合町の歴史]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:河合町の歴史]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:馬見古墳群]] into [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=[[File:Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front part on the right, rear part in the back left) |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ōtsukayama Cluster in the northern group) |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[Kitakatsuragi District]], [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 35|coordinates = 9.4 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 44|coordinates = 34.1 |omitted = JP-29 |type=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:河合の Municipal History]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:河合の Municipal History]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:河合の Municipal History]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - 21:16, * [[Scallop-shaped Kofun]] ...g|thumb|right|270px|[[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]])]] ...is exemplified by [[:en:Otomeyama Kofun|Otomeyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], while the second type is represented by {{ill|Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|ja|... 9 KB (1,000 words)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akabori Chausuyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - 07:55, * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ...{{nihongo|[[Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)|Ōtsukayama Kofun]]|[[:ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)|大塚山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|Shiroyama Kofun|城山古墳}}, {{nihongo|Takayamazuka No.1, 2, 3, 4 ... 90 KB (10,125 words) - 18:55, 10 {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大塚山古墳 (河合町)|version=104091592|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]][[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]]}} [[sn:Ōtsukayama Kofun (Kawai, Nara)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳 (河合町)]] [[ja:大塚山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawai Town]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q132170172}} |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = {{Coord|34.585944|N|135.742806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17227934]] Wikidata name: Kawai Otsukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |image=File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg |name=Ōtsukayama Kofun | part_of={{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q132170172}} |alternate_name=Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun |location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Kitakatsuragi District|Kitakatsuragi District]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], Kawai |coordinates = {{Coord|34.585944|N|135.742806|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-29 |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (Keyhole-shaped mound) |area=Total length: 215m (197m)
Height: 16.9m (front part) |built=[[:en:5th century|5th century]] late |burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]・[[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |史跡=National [[Historic Site of Japan]] "Ōtsukayama Kofun" (part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group") |Special Notes=Ranked 27th largest in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html Ranking of kofun by size (Japan)] (Sakai City Website, updated May 13, 2018). |location map = {{location map|Japan Nara|map_dot_label=Ōtsukayama Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=9.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=44|lon_sec=34.1|mark=前方後円墳-南南東.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|35|9.4|N|135|44|34.1|E}}}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:790911 K Ohtsukayama Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|190px|right|Aerial view of Ōtsukayama Kofun.]] '''Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (大塚山古墳), also known as '''Kawai Ōtsukayama Kofun''' (川合大塚山古墳), is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai Town]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The mound is a keyhole-shaped kofun and is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Cluster|lt=Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (northern group: Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster). It is Historic Site1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan]] as part of the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group." == Overview == The kofun has the following area: * Total length: 215 meters{{Sfn|Ōtsuka|Kobayashi|Kumano|1989|p=117}}, or 197 meters[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/kanko/kankou_rekishi/midokoro/kofun.html Kofun] - Kawai Town Website. * Rear mound diameter: 108 meters; height: 15.8 meters * Front mound width: 123 meters; height: 16.9 meters Located in Kawai Town, the keyhole-shaped mound faces south and consists of three tiers. The mound is well-preserved, with the surrounding moat, now converted to rice fields, still leaving clear traces of its shield type. As part of the Mami Kofun Group, it is situated in the northern section, within the Ōtsukayama Kofun Cluster. This cluster contains eight kofun, including keyhole-shaped, square-shaped, and round-shaped mounds. Ōtsukayama Kofun is the largest and most central of these. Since no excavations have been conducted, the burial facilities remain unknown. The mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (paving stones) and surrounded by cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]. burial goods recovered include cylindrical haniwa, morning-glory-shaped haniwa, and figurative haniwa (e.g., house-shaped haniwa with small shields, shield-shaped haniwa, and lid-shaped haniwa), as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]-style Haji pottery.[http://www.town.kawai.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/27/hokutobu.pdf Historic Site of Northeastern Kawai Town] - Kawai Town Website. The inclusion of Sue pottery indicates the mound was constructed in the mid to late 5th century, making it the largest kofun in the Nara Basin during that period. On December 28, 1956, the Ōtsukayama Kofun and seven other nearby mounds were collectively Historic Site1 as the "Ōtsukayama Kofun Group," a [[National Historic Site of Japan]]. Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Top of the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front mound to the rear mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-1.jpg|View from the rear mound to the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Stele at the top of the front mound Kawai Otsukayama Kofun, hori.jpg|Moat 川合大塚山古墳出土 家形埴輪片 (盾付き円柱).JPG|House-shaped haniwa fragment (cylindrical with shields)
{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} - {{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum.}} == Incident == On April 27, 2020, part of the mound was dug up, and two cylindrical haniwa were stolen. By May 1, fragments of one haniwa were returned to the mound. By May 28, the other haniwa was reassembled using adhesive and returned to the mound. Such actions—altering, collecting, or moving [[Historic Site of Japan]] without authorization—constitute a violation of the [[Historic Site Protection Law of Japan]] (Articles 125, 196, and 197) and were under investigation as criminal damage.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO59953760T00C20A6000000/|title=奈良の古墳、埴輪盗まれる 犯人が戻す?現場で発見|work=[[Nihon Keizai Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-04}}{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/325AC0100000214#AA|title=文化財保護法 第13章 罰則|work=Historic Site Protection Law|accessdate=2020-06-19}} On June 12, 2020, the town's Board of Education announced that local children digging for bamboo shoots, along with their guardians, were responsible.{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.asahi.com/sp/articles/ASN6D6QCMN6DPOMB00Q.html|title=埴輪持ち去りはタケノコ掘りの親子ら 接着剤で組み立て|work=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2020-06-13}} == notes == == References == {{small|（References used in article writing）}} *{{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ōtsuka|first=Hatsushige|last2=Kobayashi|first2=Saburō|last3=Kumano|first3=Masaya|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|date=1989-09|page=117|ref=harv}} == Related Literature == {{small|（Unreferenced related literature）}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|editor=|author=|year=2024|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/139328 Basic Plan for Preservation of the Ōtsukayama Kofun Group Historic Site]|publisher=Kawai Town Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} - Link via the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site "Site Reports of Japan." == See Also == * [[:en:Shimanoyama Kofun|Shimanoyama Kofun]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kawai Otsukayama Kofun}} * {{国cultural property等データベース|401|1995|Ōtsukayama Kofun Group}} * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], Kashiba, and Yamatotakada, Nara Prefecture ...tern area of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], spanning [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai, Nara]], [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Kashiba, Nara|Kashiba]], and {{ill|Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatotaka ... 6 KB (745 words)|qq=}} -
